_trigger: Aura trigger — the buff-window reader (WA's UI calls it 'Aura'; implemented internally as BuffTrigger2.lua, cited below as source only — not our name for it)
_why: The single most-used reader in real authorship (66% of the corpus) but function-driven, so absent from index.json's prototype scan. This closes that gap. A WA reads native buff-window state and flattens it; this names the fields that do the reading. See memory/aura-trigger-schema-and-matching + wa-reads-and-flattens-native-state.
_provenance: Fields + corpus_pct from 319 aura2 triggers in the OUR-WA corpus (plane/diagnose.py lens). Value-domains referenced to wa_index/value_domains.json (already sourced from WA's own Types.lua via extract.lua) where a shared table exists; else the inline domain is confirmed against WeakAuras/BuffTrigger2.lua on disk. Residue fields (subeventPrefix/Suffix, itemName/itemSlot, powertype, castType, genericShowOn, use_showgcd, form — cross-type leftovers) are deliberately EXCLUDED.

_idioms.show_on_missing_is_huge: matchesShowOn showOnActive 82 vs showOnMissing 76 — ~45% of buff auras fire when the buff is GONE (the 'you dropped it' reminder)
_idioms.gate_idioms: stacks with '>=' (at least N), remaining with '<' (about to expire)

by_name.role: match by a display NAME or by an ID placed in this field ('ID-in-names') — either catches the whole rank FAMILY (all ranks). THE all-ranks catch. COA idiom (grounded): the Stormbringer aura puts 804591 in auranames.

by_exact_id.role: match an EXACT spellId — pins ONE rank only (brittle for rank-variant abilities; corpus over-uses it)

ignore_names.role: EXCLUDE names — solves over-catch (e.g. Plague catching Plague Bolt)

resolve._role: which of the matches to use, when several

match_count.role: gate on HOW MANY auras match

per_unit_mode.role: for multi-unit scans: affected / unaffected / all

combine.role: aggregate matches across units into one state

clones.role: emit one dynamic clone per matching unit

preferred_match.role: runtime tiebreaker among currently-ACTIVE matches on the unit — 'Highest spell ID' picks among what's LIVE (what the player actually has), NOT a static highest-rank-in-DBC lookup. Rarely needed: catch-by-name already shows whatever rank is on you.

resolve._rank_resolution: CORRECTED by corpus inspection (2026-07-12): the rank answer is the CATCH method, not preferred_match (used 0/319). ALL ranks = by_name with a name OR an ID (ID-in-names catches the family — the COA idiom, 804591 in auranames). ONE rank = by_exact_id. preferred_match is only a runtime tiebreaker over active matches. Same quality gradient as [[diagnosis-tool-and-corpus-findings]]: corpus over-uses brittle exact-id.

show._role: when the aura displays (the show-axis — distinct from catch)

matchesShowOn.role: present / MISSING / by match-count / always — showOnMissing = dropped-buff reminder (domain confirmed vs BuffTrigger2.lua)

read._role: the native state it FLATTENS — exposed as trigger fields for conditions/display

tooltip.role: read numeric values out of the aura tooltip

cast_by_player.role: filter/read: cast by a player character vs an NPC

read._auto_state: exposed automatically as trigger state on a match (BuffTrigger2.lua UpdateMatchData): name, icon, stacks, debuffClass, duration, expirationTime, unitCaster, spellId, isStealable, isBossDebuff, isCastByPlayer, progressType.
